claim: Claude Code usage metrics (tokens, cost, ROI) can be tracked via CLI (npx ccusage); time saved (30-50 hrs/month typical) is the true ROI metric, not API costs
actionable: Run npx ccusage to track Claude Code ROI; focus on engineer time saved rather than absolute API costs when evaluating value
source_title: 3 ODDLY Useful Claude Code Repos No One Talks About

claim: Claude Trace allows inspection of Claude Code's system prompt, complete tool definitions, and full conversation traces including sub-agent calls and raw JSON requests/responses
actionable: Use Claude Trace to understand system prompts and tool definitions; inspect how Claude Code fires sub-agents and tools to identify improvement opportunities
source_title: 3 ODDLY Useful Claude Code Repos No One Talks About

claim: Understanding tool definitions in agent systems (via Claude Trace) enables new ways to leverage agents; tool definitions encode agent capabilities and constraints
actionable: Inspect agent tool definitions to understand what's possible; reverse-engineer tool descriptions to prompt agents more effectively
source_title: 3 ODDLY Useful Claude Code Repos No One Talks About

claim: MCP servers like Snap-Happy give agents desktop visibility (screenshot, list windows); agents can now see and interact with user's screen for richer task context
actionable: Connect desktop screenshot MCP servers to Claude Code; agents can now see what's on screen and reference visual context when assisting
source_title: 3 ODDLY Useful Claude Code Repos No One Talks About

claim: Mastering your tools compounds impact: in agentic systems, each action is multiplied by agents; understanding tool limitations and capabilities exponentially impacts productivity
actionable: Spend time learning your agentic tools deeply (trace executions, read system prompts, understand tool definitions); the time investment pays exponentially in agent leverage
